    Perfect.<channel|>The extinction of the dinosaurs remains one of the most fascinating mysteries in science. These massive creatures ruled the Earth for millions of years. However, about 66 million years ago, they suddenly vanished from the planet. Scientists have proposed several theories to explain this massive biological collapse. Understanding this event helps us learn about Earth's history.

The most widely accepted theory involves a massive asteroid impact. This celestial body struck the Yucatan Peninsula with incredible force. It created a massive crater and released a huge cloud of dust. This dust blocked out sunlight for several years. Plants died without light, causing a collapse of the food chain.

Some researchers also point toward intense volcanic activity. The Deccan Traps in modern-day India erupted repeatedly. These eruptions released vast amounts of carbon dioxide and sulfur. This led to extreme global warming and acid rain. The environment became increasingly hostile for large reptiles.

It is likely that both events worked together to cause the extinction. The combination of an impact and volcanic stress was devastating. Only a few species were able to survive these harsh conditions. Small mammals and birds eventually filled the empty ecological niches. Today, we study these fossils to piece together the past.<turn|>
